answer:
**The Falsafa corpus includes *Slokāntara* (unknown-slokantara-d7a628), a single-chapter Medieval Kawi prose work (~17k words) on dharma, sins, rebirth, charity, and social duties.**[^1]

After searching the English translation for terms like "tongue", "speech", "word", "talk", "misuse", "sin/sins", and "worst" (yielding zero direct hits), reviewing wiki cards for key passages, and reading the full chapter, **no such passage exists**. The text discusses sins (*pātaka*, *mahāpātaka*, etc., e.g., killing Brahmins, theft, slander to kings as a heinous crime), permitted falsehoods (e.g., jest, protecting life/property), and sweet vs. harsh speech,[^2] but nothing frames tongue/speech misuse as *the worst* sin.

If this refers to a different work (e.g., a Sanskrit *smṛti* like *Manusmṛti*, which covers verbal sins like slander/falsehood but not as "worst"), the corpus lacks a match. Not every topic is covered.[^3]

[^1]: *Slokāntara*, ch.1, [full chapter](/works/unknown-slokantara-d7a628/01/translation/).

[^2]: E.g., [p-200f22] on slander (*rāja-piśuna*) as heinous; [p-77e620] on non-sinful lies including jest/protection; [p-e303ea] on sweet speech ([/works/unknown-slokantara-d7a628/01/translation/#p-e303ea]).

[^3]: *Slokāntara* wiki card ([/works/unknown-slokantara-d7a628/wiki/_work.card.md]).
